other_observers: Urling Kingery found the bird; also seen by Hugh Kingery, Glenn Walbek
duration: 30 minutes 1st day; longer two other days.
description: Urling first noticed the bird perched in willows. She first noticed lack of spotting on back. Then she saw brown head, brownish-black body without spotting in wings except for a white patch at the fore edge of the wings. Rusty sides, white breast and belly. And it called with a different note than Spotted Towhees.
